name: Soberton
overview: Soberton is a village in the Meon Valley in southern Hampshire, within the South Downs National Park, lying east of the A32 a few miles south of Droxford. Recorded in the Domesday Book as 'Sudbertone', its centre features a War Memorial, the early 16th-century Church of St Peter and St Paul (part of one of the largest parishes in the UK), and the 17th-century White Lion pub. A vault beneath the church was reputedly used to store contraband on an 18th-century smuggling route, and the Georgian manor Soberton Towers stands north of the church.
character: A quiet, historic chalk-stream village in the South Downs, prized for its rural calm, country pubs and access to the Meon Valley Trail.
amenities: The White Lion (17th-century country pub with B&B)
Church of St Peter and St Paul (early 16th century)
Soberton Village Hall (function room for up to 200)
Meon Valley Trail (former railway) for walking/cycling/riding
War Memorial at the village centre

schools.name: Meon Valley primary schools (e.g. Droxford Junior, Meonstoke Infant)

prices.summary: A sought-after South Downs village; character cottages, period homes and some larger houses, with limited supply keeping values firm. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling the Meon Valley Trail
Church of St Peter and St Paul and its smuggling history
Walks to Old Winchester Hill and Butser Hill
Chalk-stream walks along the River Meon
placesToEat: The White Lion (AA Rosette country pub)
pros: Beautiful, tranquil South Downs/Meon Valley setting
Direct access to the Meon Valley Trail
Rich local history and period character
cons: Very limited everyday amenities (no clear village shop or school in the village itself)
Quiet/remote feel may not suit everyone
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a peaceful, historic Meon Valley village with great pubs and walking, who don't need in-village shops or schools.
primarySupermarket: Shops in nearby Droxford, Wickham and Bishop's Waltham
